title: The Sponge

jokes.text: If you see someone with a hearing aid...you don't think, 'Oh, they must have been listening real good. Yeah, to a lot of important stuff.' No, they're deaf.
jokes.setup: Building parallel between glasses making people look intelligent and hearing aids
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by pointing out hearing aids indicate deafness, not good listening
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with strong logical structure and unexpected turn. Well-crafted comparison that reveals absurdity in common thinking.

jokes.text: It's hard enough living next door.
jokes.setup: Kramer saying he couldn't live with himself if he didn't help fight AIDS
jokes.punchline: George's dry comment about living next door to Kramer being hard enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect George response - understated and character-specific. Economy of language with solid payoff.

jokes.text: If I date a taking person...everyone's taking, taking, taking. No one's giving. It's bedlam.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he should date a giving person since he's a taking person
jokes.punchline: Escalating to 'bedlam' for simple relationship dynamics
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Jerry logic - overthinking simple concepts with dramatic language. 'Bedlam' is perfectly excessive.

jokes.text: I'd just come from buying a speedboat.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his lie about how he got the number
jokes.punchline: Random, completely irrelevant detail about speedboat purchase
jokes.explanation: Jerry's casual absurdity - the speedboat is random enough to be believable as a distraction tactic.

jokes.text: Haven't been in the lab yet.
jokes.setup: George asking about sexual chemistry
jokes.punchline: Elaine treating 'chemistry' literally as laboratory work
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay, economical delivery. Typical Elaine wit with scientific metaphor.

jokes.text: I think it began with a W, maybe a Q.
jokes.setup: Jerry on phone explaining he doesn't remember the name of person who gave him the number
jokes.punchline: Suggesting a name could begin with Q, which is absurdly uncommon
jokes.explanation: Minor absurdist touch - Q names are rare enough to sound ridiculous as a guess.

jokes.text: He scratches off a 32 and he puts in '31.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jerry's thin appearance and 31 waist
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jerry's pathetic vanity in altering his jean labels
jokes.explanation: Perfectly petty character detail that's both pathetic and relatable. Sets up major callback later.

jokes.text: Well, this is the Jerry Seinfeld that only I know.
jokes.setup: George revealing Jerry's secret about the jeans
jokes.punchline: George presenting himself as having special insider knowledge
jokes.explanation: George's self-importance about knowing Jerry's pathetic secret. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I'll be cut out of the loop.
jokes.setup: George explaining why he can't keep Jerry's secrets
jokes.punchline: George's dramatic fear of social exclusion over gossip
jokes.explanation: George's neurotic priorities - values gossip access over friendship loyalty.

jokes.text: I'm gonna do a hard target search of every drug store, general store...health store, grocery store in a 25 block radius
jokes.setup: Elaine learning the Sponge is off the market
jokes.punchline: Military-style operation language for birth control shopping
jokes.explanation: Elaine's characteristic obsessiveness with action movie terminology. Good escalation of stakes.

jokes.text: Figure it's something.
jokes.setup: Asked what birth control Susan uses
jokes.punchline: George's hilariously vague and unconcerned response
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - completely oblivious to important relationship details. Maximum comedy in minimal words.

jokes.text: I got a 31 waist, mister.
jokes.setup: Jerry challenging Kramer about being in better shape
jokes.punchline: Jerry citing his fake waist size as proof
jokes.explanation: Callback works because audience knows Jerry's lie. Adds ironic layer to his boasting.

jokes.text: Sixty? Just give me the whole case, I'll be on my way.
jokes.setup: Elaine's escalating Sponge purchase from 3 to 10 to 20 to 25
jokes.punchline: Finally giving up and buying the entire 60-sponge case
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation sequence showing Elaine's panic-buying spiral. Well-structured build-up.

jokes.text: Yeah, the oceans are really getting very sudsy.
jokes.setup: Lena explaining her biodegradable detergent doesn't pollute oceans
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic response about sudsy oceans
jokes.explanation: Jerry's trademark sarcasm about environmental concerns. Good visual absurdity with 'sudsy oceans.'

jokes.text: Do the bums ever complain, 'Soup again?'
jokes.setup: Learning soup kitchen serves soup at 6am
jokes.punchline: Jerry imagining homeless people as picky customers
jokes.explanation: Dark but not mean-spirited. Jerry's perspective on social issues through selfish lens.

jokes.text: What does a poet need an unlisted number for?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Maya Angelou being hard to reach
jokes.punchline: Jerry's assumption poets shouldn't need privacy
jokes.explanation: Typical Jerry logic about profession-based expectations. Mild but consistent with character.

jokes.text: You know, you use the: The what? You know, the:
jokes.setup: George claiming to know Susan's birth control method
jokes.punchline: George completely unable to name anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - caught in lie, fumbling pathetically. Physical comedy through dialogue.

jokes.text: There's really no big surprises here...so make-up sex is all that I have left.
jokes.setup: George explaining his relationship situation to get a Sponge
jokes.punchline: Admitting his sex life is predictable and boring
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest George admitting relationship mediocrity. Perfectly pathetic and relatable.

jokes.text: I'm sure you'll have another fight, George.
jokes.setup: George's desperate plea for Sponge for make-up sex
jokes.punchline: Elaine casually assuming George will fight with Susan again
jokes.explanation: Elaine's casual dismissal is perfectly cold. Good contrast to George's desperation.

jokes.text: You're lucky you're walking out of here with a pair of pants on!
jokes.setup: Kramer apparently winning big at poker
jokes.punchline: Threat about taking someone's pants
jokes.explanation: Generic gambling threat but fits the escalated stakes context. Moderate impact.

jokes.text: If you were in the Mafia, would you tell her every time you killed someone?
jokes.setup: Jerry angry at George for breaking confidence
jokes.punchline: Comparing gossip to murder confession
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd escalation. Jerry making minor betrayal equivalent to murder confession.

jokes.text: You can't have sex with someone you admire.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his problem with Lena being too good
jokes.punchline: Jerry's twisted relationship philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark insight about relationships. Quotable, character-specific, and disturbingly relatable. High craft across all dimensions.

jokes.text: I just couldn't decide if he was really spongeworthy.
jokes.setup: Elaine didn't use a Sponge despite seeming ready
jokes.punchline: Creating 'spongeworthy' as relationship criteria
jokes.explanation: Iconic Seinfeld neologism. 'Spongeworthy' became cultural shorthand. Original concept with perfect execution.

jokes.text: You know what you are? You're a ribbon bully.
jokes.setup: Volunteers insisting Kramer must wear AIDS ribbon
jokes.punchline: Kramer's characterization of ribbon enforcement as bullying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic and terminology. 'Ribbon bully' is both accurate and absurd.

jokes.text: It's like Beat the Clock. There's a lot of pressure there.
jokes.setup: George explaining why he hates condoms
jokes.punchline: Comparing condom opening to game show pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurosis. Game show reference makes intimate moment comically mechanical.

jokes.text: And if I can speak frankly, I'm actually quite good at it.
jokes.setup: Billy making his case for being spongeworthy
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual boasting
jokes.explanation: Adequately uncomfortable but predictable. Standard cringe humor without special insight.

jokes.text: She is depraved.
jokes.setup: Jerry discovers Lena's bedroom closet (implied to contain kinky items)
jokes.punchline: Jerry's delighted realization about Lena's hidden nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff. Jerry's joy at discovering depravity is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I change the 32 waist on the label...to a 31 on all my jeans.
jokes.setup: Jerry deciding to confess something to Lena
jokes.punchline: Revealing his pathetic vanity secret
jokes.explanation: Major callback payoff. Jerry's confession is perfectly timed and pathetic.

jokes.text: It's too late.
jokes.setup: George and Susan struggling with condom wrapper
jokes.punchline: George's defeat by packaging
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to George's earlier complaints. Maximum pathos in two words.

jokes.text: I guess we're just going to have to teach him to wear the ribbon.
jokes.setup: Kramer refusing to wear the ribbon
jokes.punchline: Ominous threat about ribbon compliance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of ribbon conflict. Menacing language for trivial issue.

jokes.text: She said I wasn't spongeworthy. Wouldn't waste a Sponge on me.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining what happened with Lena
jokes.punchline: Jerry being rejected based on sponge criteria
jokes.explanation: Good use of established concept. Jerry failing the sponge test is ironically fitting.

jokes.text: It's probably so the woman has one last chance to change her mind.
jokes.setup: Discussion about difficult condom wrappers
jokes.punchline: Jerry's cynical theory about packaging design
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark observational humor. Plausible conspiracy theory with relationship insight.

jokes.text: I wish I could help you, but I can't afford two of them.
jokes.setup: Billy wanting second round of sex
jokes.punchline: Elaine rationing Sponges even after successful sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback. Elaine's extreme rationing even during good sex shows obsessive logic.

tmdb_overview: Elaine holds a monopoly on the contraceptive devices that Susan prefers; Kramer refuses to wear a ribbon on his AIDS walk.
